_instructions: This file draws the 'Upper Logan Canyon' schematic page (Franklin Basin down to the UWRL Water Lab).
See public/schematics/README.md for the full field reference and a longer walkthrough - this is the quick version.
NODES: each node is a card on the diagram, listed in the order the water actually flows.
id         - unique within this file. Tributaries/diversions/links reference another node's id via connectsTo.
name       - for kind 'mainstem'/'tributary'/'diversion', must fuzzy-match a live station's display name from
HydroServer/DWRi/USGS (partial matches work, e.g. 'Beaver Creek' will match 'Beaver Creek: Above Diversion').
For 'junction'/'link' nodes it is just label text.
kind       - 'mainstem' (a real gauge station on the main channel), 'junction' (an invisible confluence point on the main
channel, no card - where a tributary/diversion attaches), 'tributary' (water flowing IN), 'diversion' (water
flowing OUT), or 'link' (a card with no live data of its own that just navigates to another schematic page).
connectsTo - tributary/diversion/link only: the id of the mainstem/junction node this attaches to, or another
tributary/diversion/link's id to chain onto. Omit it for a 'link' node that's just the next stop on the
main channel (see ext_to_lower below) rather than a side attachment.
side       - tributary/diversion/link only, when connectsTo is set: 'left' or 'right', which side of the main channel
to draw the card on.
colorGroup - optional; must match a name from waterway_groups or schematic_groups in config/station_config.yaml, used
to tint the card's background.
linkTo     - optional on any node; the schematic page slug to navigate to on click. On a normal node this adds a
'view full system' button below its card; on a 'link' node it's the entire point of the card.
label      - linkTo button text override (defaults to name).
There's no separate edges list - the main channel line, every tributary/diversion's connector, and flow direction (in
for tributary, out for diversion) are all derived automatically from kind + connectsTo + the order nodes appear above.
This page continues into 'Lower Logan River' via the 'ext_to_lower' link node at the bottom - do not remove it without
also updating lower-logan.json's matching recap section.
Sites displayed here are the ones at and above the UWRL Water Lab: the natural tributaries (Beaver Creek, Ricks Spring,
Temple Fork, Right Hand Fork, Dewitt Springs) plus the USGS Cache Highline Canal and Above First Dam stations.
title: Upper Logan River: Canyon
subtitle: Franklin Basin down to the UWRL Water Lab
